task_completion.description: The agent opened the AuPbSn40.jpg image and defined at least 6 square ROIs (3 for Phase A, 3 for Phase B).
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji window showing the AuPbSn40.jpg image with multiple square ROIs drawn. The ROI Manager should be visible listing at least 6 ROIs specifically named PhaseA_1, PhaseA_2, PhaseA_3, PhaseB_1, PhaseB_2, and PhaseB_3.

task_completion.description: The ROI set is saved to rois.zip in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer, terminal window, or save dialog showing 'rois.zip' located in '~/Fiji_Data/results/texture/'.

task_completion.description: Texture features are computed for the ROIs and saved to glcm_measurements.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results table in ImageJ showing texture measurements (like ASM, Contrast, Correlation, IDM, Entropy) for the ROIs, AND a file explorer or terminal showing 'glcm_measurements.csv' in the results directory.

task_completion.description: An annotated overlay image showing the ROI locations on the microstructure is generated and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An ImageJ window showing the image with ROIs permanently drawn/flattened on it, OR a file explorer/terminal showing 'annotated_microstructure.png' in the results directory.

task_completion.description: A summary report is written to texture_report.txt containing required statistical comparisons and conclusions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'texture_report.txt'. The text must include statistical comparisons (Mean ± StdDev) for Phase A and Phase B, identification of the best discriminating feature, and a conclusion about distinguishing the phases.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI to define ROIs and compute features, rather than generating mock files via scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the ImageJ/Fiji interface open with the image, ROI Manager, and Results table actively being used.

integrity.description: The ROIs are placed on visually distinct regions corresponding to the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the ImageJ window or annotated image, Phase A ROIs should be placed on the brighter, finer-textured lamellar regions, and Phase B ROIs should be placed on the darker, coarser-textured regions.

integrity.description: The report contains actual calculated numerical values rather than placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The texture_report.txt file contains specific, realistic numerical values for Mean and StdDev, indicating genuine calculations were performed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image AuPbSn40.jpg is an 8-bit grayscale image (600x412 pixels) showing a eutectic alloy. Phase A is a brighter, fine-textured lamellar phase, while Phase B consists of darker, coarser-textured regions. The task requires exactly 4 output files in ~/Fiji_Data/results/texture/: 1) rois.zip (containing at least 6 ROIs named PhaseA_1..3 and PhaseB_1..3), 2) glcm_measurements.csv (with a header and >=6 rows of data), 3) annotated_microstructure.png (showing the ROIs on the image), and 4) texture_report.txt (containing Mean ± StdDev, best feature, and conclusion).
